description: Conversation consisting entirely of rhetorical questions and complaints about meetings. No events are scheduled — the whole point is questioning whether meetings should exist at all.

mock_llm_response.summary: No actionable calendar events found. The conversation consists entirely of rhetorical questions ('who even has time?', 'should we even bother?', 'why are we doing this?') expressing anti-meeting sentiment. No scheduling intent exists.

notes: Rhetorical question pattern. Questions like 'who has time for meetings?' and 'should we even bother?' are expressions of frustration, not requests to schedule. The conclusion is explicitly anti-scheduling.
